ASBESTOS CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-17-2020 Set For Trial On Nov-02-20 At 11:15 A.M. In Dept 503

Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff (Added To Calendar For Issuance Of Tentative Ruling Only)
Off Calendar Jul-16-2020 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: (PART 2 OF 2)
(Mini Minutes continued from previous entry)

No later than July 30, 2020 plaintiffs must provide a statement to each defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims as to each defendant. No later than July 30, 2020 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. The asbestos case management conference set for August 20, 2020 is vacated. A new case management conference is set for September 17, 2020 at 1:30 p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than September 18, 2020 2020. Responses to the demand are due no later than October 2, 2020. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands/offers stating their best and final settlement effort. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via electronic mail by October 23, 2020 at 12:00 p.m. Mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, October 29, 2020 at 1:30 p.m. in 603. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is granted excuse based on good cause. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed order in compliance with C.R.C. 3.1312. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Jul-16-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jason M. Rose of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing through CourtCall for plaintiffs and moving parties.Carol Esterlin, as Successor-In-Interest; Gary Esterlin, Brian Esterlin, and Carol Esterlin, as Wrongful Death Heir of Gary Esterlin, Sr., Deceased.
Constance Fraenkel of SPANOS | PRZETAK (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel appearing through CourtCall.
Jennifer A. Cormier of MANNING, GROSS & MASSENBURG (415) 512-4381, appearing through CourtCall for defendant Oscar E. Erickson, Inc.
Jon C. James of HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing through CourtCall for defendant Foster Wheeler.
Jocelyn M. Soriano of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing through CourtCall for defendant Riley Power.

RULING: PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF
Not contested and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). Defendant Oscar E. Erickson, Inc. filed a conditional opposition. The trial date is November 2, 2020 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is November 13, 2020. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is October 29, 2020. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is October 16, 2020. Expert discovery cut-off date is October 30, 2020. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than August 20, 2020. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
(Mini Minutes continued, see next entry)
